root_text.sn35.163:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā mahākoṭṭhiko …pe… bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.163:1.3: “Yaṁ kho, koṭṭhika, dukkhaṁ tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.163:1.5: Cakkhu kho, koṭṭhika, dukkhaṁ; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.163:1.6: Rūpā dukkhā; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.163:1.7: Cakkhuviññāṇaṁ dukkhaṁ; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.163:1.8: Cakkhusamphasso dukkho; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.163:1.9: Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi dukkhaṁ; tatra te chando pahātabbo …pe…
root_text.sn35.163:1.10: jivhā dukkhā; tatra te chando pahātabbo …pe…
root_text.sn35.163:1.11: mano dukkho; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.163:1.12: Dhammā dukkhā; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.163:1.13: Manoviññāṇaṁ dukkhaṁ; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.163:1.14: Manosamphasso dukkho; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.163:1.15: Yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi dukkhaṁ; tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.163:1.16: Yaṁ kho, koṭṭhika, dukkhaṁ tatra te chando pahātabbo”ti.

translation_text.sn35.163:1.1: Then Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita … said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn35.163:1.2: “Sir, may the Buddha please teach me Dhamma in brief. When I’ve heard it, I’ll live alone, withdrawn, diligent, keen, and resolute.”
translation_text.sn35.163:1.3: “Koṭṭhita, you should give up desire for what is suffering.
translation_text.sn35.163:1.8: and eye contact are suffering: you should give up desire for them.
translation_text.sn35.163:1.9: The pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact is also suffering; you should give up desire for it.
translation_text.sn35.163:1.14: and mind contact are suffering: you should give up desire for them.
translation_text.sn35.163:1.15: The pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact is also suffering: you should give up desire for it.
translation_text.sn35.163:1.16: Koṭṭhita, you should give up desire for what is suffering.”
